Ad Text:
About the Role

We are looking for a seasoned Senior Business Analyst to join a high-profile Project Management Office (PMO) tasked with reshaping the ERP landscape for smaller government entities. This isn't just about maintaining the status quo; you will be at the forefront of a shift toward competitive, agile non-SAP ERP solutions.

In this role, you'll dig deep into business processes, translating complex requirements into clear, actionable specifications for modern, cloud-based systems. You will be the bridge between technical teams and business stakeholders, ensuring that data goals and project objectives are perfectly aligned.

What You'll Be Doing

Leading Workshops: Facilitating sessions with various business areas to uncover diverse requirements and identify potential hurdles to new solutions.
Defining the Future State: Documenting business needs and success criteria while mapping out "to-be" processes.
Process Optimisation: Working both independently and collaboratively to re-design business and data processes, driving efficiency through better information management.
Stakeholder Management: Collaborating with subject matter experts, architects, and developers to ensure the chosen solution hits the mark.
Delivering Insights: Using your analytical prowess to interpret data and provide the insights needed for informed, high-level decision-making.

Your Experience & Expertise

The successful candidate must bring a record with non-SAP ERP products. We are specifically looking for professionals who can evaluate and recommend modern systems such as:

TechnologyOne
Workday
Microsoft Dynamics 365

Core Requirements:

End-to-End BA Lifecycle: Extensive experience leading the BA process from initial design through to implementation.
ERP Implementation: Hands-on experience defining requirements specifically for ERP uplift projects.
Process Re-design: Strong skills in business process mapping and re-design.
Government Context: Experience within a Shared Services environment or a government PMO is highly regarded.

Security Clearance

Baseline Security Clearance.

Collaborate with Commercial and Consumer Pricing Managers to deliver business objectives
Provide technical support on model methodologies and their implementation
Sydney OR Melbourne CBD location – hybrid office/ work from home
Permanent role

The purpose of the Senior Pricing Advisor role is to execute business modelling for pricing initiatives in SME Packages, Commercial Motor, Residential Strata, and consulting projects. The role also involves proposing, developing, and actioning pricing insights to enable SME Packages, Commercial Motor and Residential Strata products to achieve financial targets.

What you’ll do:

Lead communication with key stakeholders and lead/mentor junior members of the team in their analytical work and career development
Develop pricing recommendations and proposals designed to closely align pricing strategy to each of the brand’s budgets and implement price changes timely and accurately within the business/function
Execute commercial data capability by maintaining existing Commercial pricing infrastructure as well as supporting the build of modelling ready datasets
Lead pricing and senior pricing analysts to model products and covers within SME/CI Motor/Strata as other members focus on BAU deliverables  
Lead pricing initiatives to explore, develop both internal and external data/modelling capabilities.

What you’ll bring:

Minimum of 5 years of relevant experience, preferably in the General Insurance industry
Proven ability to lead and engage team members to achieve individual, team and divisional goals
Strong knowledge of GI products and pricing of key business drivers
Strong knowledge of business modelling – GLMs, GBMs and other machine learning techniques
Advanced analytical skills, ability to develop solutions and courses of action by using an information base to identify key issues, compare with other data and determine cause effect relationships.

What we can offer you

Discounts of up to 25% on our various Insurance products
Flexible working environment and arrangements; genuine focus on work-life balance
Numerous discounts with our corporate partners (retail & shopping / travel & holiday / health & wellbeing)
We offer support and various programs for our people: (Employee Assistance Program (EAP), Referral Program ($600), Facilities for nursing mothers, Years of Service Recognition)

Employee benefits | Suncorp Group

If this opportunity sounds like the challenge you have been looking for please apply online today or for more information please contact Annette_frasca@suncorp.com.au

At Suncorp we build inclusion by providing an environment where everyone can be themselves and feel valued, involved and respected for their perspectives and contribution.

We believe we are at our best when our workforce is as diverse, talented and passionate as the communities in which we live and operate, and where our people feel included, valued and connected. We are passionate about inspiring our people by creating a diverse, accessible, and inclusive culture, offering flexible work, career development and internal mobility, and building connected relationships amongst our team members and with our customers.

If you require any support and adjustments to participate equitably in our recruitment process or in the workplace, we encourage you to contact Annette_frasca@suncorp.com.au for a confidential conversation.

Our client, a leading player in the Mining Equipment, Technology, and Services (METS) sector, is seeking a Technical Lead to oversee the maintenance and health of critical equipment through innovative AI, machine learning, and predictive technologies. You will lead the development team while integrating advanced solutions for machine maintenance, equipment health, fault detection, and operational efficiency.

You will bring a broad technical skill set and the ability to work across various technologies and platforms, from development to system architecture. With strong leadership skills and a problem-solving mindset, you will identify opportunities, delays, and system faults, driving solutions for improved operational performance. The role offers growth opportunities, with a potential 6-month rolling contract.

Responsibilities

Guide a team of developers while ensuring high-quality delivery and provide hands-on support as a Senior Developer, writing code and performing code reviews.

Contribute to high-level design (HLD), architectural approval, and strategic planning.

Drive the use of AI and machine learning for predictive maintenance and fault detection using Python.

Oversee data collection and analysis for faults, delays, and shutdown management to optimize equipment health and operational efficiency.

Manage system integrations involving SAP, including alarms and fault management, and lead the use of AWS, Terraform, CI/CD pipelines, and containerization in system integration.

Essential Requirements of the Candidate

Proven experience as a Technical Lead in the mining or heavy machinery sector, with strong expertise in predictive analytics and machine maintenance/equipment health.

Experience working with machine learning/AI technologies, and agentic coding, including Large Language Models.

Strong experience with Python for backend development, machine learning, and AI models.

Proven ability to lead a development team, provide technical guidance, and perform code reviews.

Experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, CI/CD processes, Terraform, and containerization is essential.

Hands-on experience with SAP integrations is also important.

Please apply directly. For in-office and potential on-site visit requirements, we are looking only for candidates based in Perth or Brisbane. Candidates must have full working rights in Australia.

ASC is Australia’s submarine company. We built the Royal Australian Navy’s Collins Class submarines, sustain them, and are now delivering their Life of Type Extension to increase the boats’ service lives. We have also been selected by the Australian Government to sustain and jointly build the nation’s nuclear-powered submarines – the largest and most complex industrial undertaking in Australia’s history.

Our work is interesting and meaningful, contributing directly to Australia’s national security. We offer rewarding roles that provide the opportunity to grow your career, and the flexibility to balance life and work. A career at ASC is like no other, delivering a deeper purpose we can all be proud of.

About the Opportunity

The EPMO Reporting Manager plays a key role in strengthening ASC’s enterprise reporting capability. In this role, you will support the Enterprise Portfolio Committee (EPC) by delivering clear, accurate and comprehensive insights that enable priority-setting across ASC’s project portfolio. Operating within a highly collaborative environment, you will partner with Business Units and Program Management Offices to ensure transparency, visibility and informed decision‑making across the organisation.

Responsibilities

Prepare, enhance and deliver high‑quality enterprise portfolio reporting.
Consolidate inputs from Business Units and PMOs to maintain a complete and accurate portfolio view.
Respond to ad‑hoc requests promptly, providing meaningful, data‑driven insights.
Advise senior leaders with clear, actionable recommendations.
Drive improvements to reporting tools, templates, models and processes.
Support uplift in enterprise data maturity, usability and transparency.

About you 

Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Power BI.
Experience accessing and ingesting data from multiple systems and sources.
Ability to translate complex information into clear, accessible insights.
Strong capability in building business‑friendly data models.
Skilled at designing interactive visualisations and telling compelling stories with data.
Excellent communication skills and ability to advise senior stakeholders.
Relevant tertiary qualifications (highly advantageous).

Eligibility

This role requires a current National Police Clearance and an Australian Defence Security Clearance. Your eligibility for this role is dependent on you obtaining and maintaining such clearance.

To check your eligibility please visit the Australian Government Security Vetting Agency website.

Benefits

Unique and challenging work with a deeper purpose.
Flexibility over how, when and where you work for the best work/life balance.
Up to 10 days additional leave every year so you can rest and recharge.
Generous parental leave so you can give your family the focus it deserves.
Subsidised childcare to ease the financial pressure on your family.
Health and wellbeing initiatives so you can perform at your best at work.
Professional development opportunities to advance your career.
A diverse and supportive environment where you can thrive.
Discounts and exclusive offers from leading Australian brands to help you save.

Adelaide, South Australia (SA), combines cosmopolitan living with natural beauty, featuring picturesque hills, renowned wineries, and stunning coastlines. As a centre for innovation, SA offers careers in defence, space and advanced manufacturing. Celebrated for its Mediterranean climate and cultural richness, Adelaide provides excellent work-life balance. Explore world-class wineries, enjoy the vibrant food scene, and experience affordable, family-friendly living in a welcoming, community-oriented city.

About ASC

Established in 1985, ASC’s 2500-plus staff offer a depth of submarine design, project management, supply chain, construction and engineering experience and expertise that is unparalleled in Australia’s defence industry. The company’s work on Australia’s nuclear-powered submarine build and the Collins Class Life of Type Extension takes place in Osborne, South Australia, with sustainment shared across Osborne and Henderson, Western Australia. ASC also plays a leading role in training Royal Australian Navy submariners.

Apply today for a career with a deeper purpose.

Early applications are encouraged as shortlisting may commence immediately.

MARS are partnering an established and evolving financial services organisation to appoint a Finance Transformation & Reporting Manager to lead the uplift of finance systems and reporting capability. This hands-on role sits at the intersection of finance, technology and process improvement, helping the business transition from Excel-based reporting to automated dashboards, scalable data solutions, and AI-enabled workflows.

The Role

Reporting to the Head of Finance, you'll own finance systems initiatives, reporting automation, and dashboard development, ensuring high-quality data flows from core systems through to the data warehouse. You'll also drive continuous improvement and emerging technology adoption while supporting statutory reporting and audit processes.

The Responsibilities:

Systems & Reporting Automation:

Lead the design, build and maintenance of automated reporting solutions and dashboards
Ensure data accuracy, completeness and integrity from source systems into the data warehouse
Transition finance processes away from Excel into scalable, automated systems

AI & Technology Enablement:

Identify and implement AI-driven solutions to enhance reporting and efficiency
Stay across emerging technologies and best practices in finance automation

Continuous Improvement:

Review, document and enhance existing finance processes and controls
Identify opportunities to standardise, streamline and automate finance activities

Year-End & Half-Year Reporting:

Prepare financial statements for designated entities
Support ESG reporting initiatives
Act as a key contact for auditors during year-end and half-year reporting

Team Support & Collaboration:

Assist with selected month-end tasks and provide coverage during peak periods
Build strong relationships across finance and the wider business


The Requirements:

CA/CPA qualified with 8+ years of experience finance experience, with strong systems/transformation coverage
Must have a blend of financial control, systems and transformation exposure, ideally from a medium-large, listed or complex organisation
Hands-on experience in finance systems, reporting, dashboards and transformation initiatives
Strong process improvement and ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously
Financial services experience highly regarded but not essential
Reliable, adaptable, deadline-focused and solutions-oriented
Strong stakeholder management and leadership skills
Interest in AI and emerging finance technologies


To Note:

They operate a hybrid working model of 3 days in the office, 2 days from home
Must be an Australian Citizen, Permanent Resident or have long-term working rights

Why Join?

This is a high-impact role with the opportunity to shape the finance function's future systems and reporting capability. You'll work on visible, transformative projects, gain exposure to senior stakeholders, and help embed long-term efficiency, automation, and innovation across the business.


If you're interested in this opportunity, please apply to the role directly. Alternatively, you can send your CV over to Tara.Magwenzi@marsrecruitment.com.au

Due to the high volume of applications we receive, we are only able to contact shortlisted candidates.

About Airwallex


Airwallex is the only unified payments and financial platform for global businesses. Powered by our unique combination of proprietary infrastructure and software, we empower over 200,000 businesses worldwide – including Brex, Rippling, Navan, Qantas, SHEIN and many more – with fully integrated solutions to manage everything from business accounts, payments, spend management and treasury, to embedded finance at a global scale.

Proudly founded in Melbourne, we have a team of over 2,000 of the brightest and most innovative people in tech across 26 offices around the globe. Valued at US$8 billion and backed by world-leading investors including T. Rowe Price, Visa, Mastercard, Robinhood Ventures, Sequoia, Salesforce Ventures, DST Global, and Lone Pine Capital, Airwallex is leading the charge in building the global payments and financial platform of the future. If you’re ready to do the most ambitious work of your career, join us.

Attributes We Value


We hire successful builders with founder-like energy who want real impact, accelerated learning, and true ownership. You bring strong role-related expertise and sharp thinking, and you’re motivated by our mission and operating principles. You move fast with good judgment, dig deep with curiosity, and make decisions from first principles, balancing speed and rigor.

You’re humble and collaborative; turn zero‑to‑one ideas into real products, and you “get stuff done” end-to-end. You use AI to work smarter and solve problems faster. Here, you’ll tackle complex, high‑visibility problems with exceptional teammates and grow your career as we build the future of global banking. If that sounds like you, let’s build what’s next.

The Product team at Airwallex is a group of passionate builders and problem-solvers who are obsessed with crafting customer-centric solutions that empower businesses to operate anywhere, anytime. We combine technical expertise with a deep understanding of our customers’ needs to design and build unified, intuitive, and scalable products. As a team, we thrive in a collaborative and fast-paced environment, constantly iterating and pushing boundaries to deliver exceptional product experiences.

This role will be part of Airwallex’s Risk Platform team, building out our Anti-Fraud capabilities to protect our customers from Payment fraud. You will be responsible for developing and implementing strategies to prevent, detect, and mitigate fraud within our products and services at global scale, as well as leading Fraud-related product initiatives from ideation to launch. You will bring a fraud product to market, and productise a fraud product add on service for Airwallex to better serve our customers. 

Responsibilities:

Lead the strategy for our Payment fraud products and prioritise the roadmap for key features and capabilities

Work with commercial strategy teams to develop and enrich our customer facing fraud management offerings

Oversee the product lifecycle of our fraud prevention capabilities and tools for our risk analytics and operation teams and customers

Work with the risk analytics team to develop and implement fraud detection and prevention solutions, and monitor their effectiveness

Lead cross-functional teams, including engineering, data science, legal and compliance teams to develop market leading products that align with business objectives and regulatory requirements.

Manage Airwallex’s team of Fraud Analysts and ensure proactive monitoring of fraud metrics, identification of patterns and trends, and gathering of insights to improve fraud prevention strategies and tooling.

Requirement:

7 to 15 years of overall professional experience

5+ Years of experience in Payments Fraud or Payments Risk Management

Experience in product management, go-to-market propositions, product design, product planning and development

Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills

Additionally, you should have:

Demonstrated high academic achievements across your Bachelor’s or higher degree in a STEM field

Experience in Fintech or other domains with high conceptual complexity

Applicant Safety Policy: Fraud and Third-Party Recruiters


To protect you from recruitment scams, please be aware that Airwallex will not ask for bank details, sensitive ID numbers (i.e. passport), or any form of payment during the application or interview process. All official communication will come from an @airwallex.com email address. Please apply only through careers.airwallex.com or our official LinkedIn page.

Airwallex does not accept unsolicited resumes from search firms/recruiters. Airwallex will not pay any fees to search firms/recruiters if a candidate is submitted by a search firm/recruiter unless an agreement has been entered into with respect to specific open position(s). Search firms/recruiters submitting resumes to Airwallex on an unsolicited basis shall be deemed to accept this condition, regardless of any other provision to the contrary.

Equal opportunity


Airwallex is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We value diversity and anyone seeking employment at Airwallex is considered based on merit, qualifications, competence and talent. We don’t regard color, religion, race, national origin, sexual orientation, ancestry, citizenship, sex, marital or family status, disability, gender, or any other legally protected status when making our hiring decisions. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know.

Your work days are brighter here.

We’re obsessed with making hard work pay off, for our people, our customers, and the world around us. As a Fortune 500 company and a leading AI platform for managing people, money, and agents, we’re shaping the future of work so teams can reach their potential and focus on what matters most. The minute you join, you’ll feel it. Not just in the products we build, but in how we show up for each other. Our culture is rooted in integrity, empathy, and shared enthusiasm. We’re in this together, tackling big challenges with bold ideas and genuine care. We look for curious minds and courageous collaborators who bring sun-drenched optimism and drive. Whether you're building smarter solutions, supporting customers, or creating a space where everyone belongs, you’ll do meaningful work with Workmates who’ve got your back. In return, we’ll give you the trust to take risks, the tools to grow, the skills to develop and the support of a company invested in you for the long haul. So, if you want to inspire a brighter work day for everyone, including yourself, you’ve found a match in Workday, and we hope to be a match for you too.

About the Team

Join our team and experience Workday! It's fun to work in a company where people truly believe in what they're doing. At Workday, we're committed to bringing passion and customer focus to the business of enterprise applications. We work hard, and we're serious about what we do. But we like to have a good time, too. In fact, we run our company with that principle in mind every day: One of our core values is fun.

Workday is the leader in enterprise-class, software-as-a-service (SaaS) solutions for managing global businesses. Our solutions combine the lower cost of ownership of SaaS with a modern approach to applications. Founded by PeopleSoft veterans Dave Duffield and Aneel Bhusri, Workday delivers Human Capital Management, Payroll, Financial Management and Worker Spend Management solutions for midsize and Fortune 500 companies.

The Data Conversion consultant works on Workday's deployment projects, with a focus on the successful implementation of Workday’s Data Conversion processes to make our deployments successful. The Data Conversion Consultant works closely with the client's project team as they extract legacy data, partnering to transform the data to Workday standards and ultimately load it into Workday. This role will gain a proficiency in Workday's Implementation methodology, bring technical knowledge to uptake Workday’s tooling, and be a good partner and consultant to our Customers, combining those skills to deliver successful deployments.

About the Role

Workday is looking for a Data Conversion Consultant who has complete project lifecycle experience in a data conversion role. This role is within Workday’s Professional Services (PS) industries, and spans both Human Capital Management and Financials domains.

A Data Conversion Consultant will be responsible for ensuring the successful implementation of Workday’s Data Conversion processes on customer projects, helping establish and champion best practices for all deployments.

You will be the primary point of contact for data conversion between the project team and customers, working directly with customers, functional consultants and integration consultants to establish conversion approach, complete migration of configuration and to complete the data conversion / load of customer data into Workday.

Assist customers with their data extract, explaining the conversion process, defining data requirements, and working with them to accurate data in their legacy systems and prepare for the data conversion cycle.

Demonstrate strong customer focus alongside organizational and consulting skills that enable both a successful engagement and customer satisfaction.

About You

Basic Qualifications

8+ years of ERP solutions experience (SAP, Oracle, PeopleSoft, NetSuite, Lawson, etc.) as either a consultant or customer

5+ years of data migration, integration development, report building, or software development

Ability to obtain, hold and maintain a security clearance to the required level, is an inherent requirement for this role

Other Qualifications

Understand technical data conversion issues, expertly resolve issues and communicate solutions.

Functional expert in one or more Workday functional areas (e.g., HRMS, Payroll, Financials).

Proficient with ETL, SQL/MySQL, XML, Microsoft Excel, stored procedures or other data tooling

Experience working with customers and/or team members and leading data requirements sessions

Participation in a minimum of 1 full life cycle software implementation

Experience with packaged HRMS/Financial systems is preferred.

Understanding of the complete software implementation lifecycle - including data requirements gathering, dataset migration, data reconciliation, and move to production

Experience in a Data Conversion work stream on a software implementation

Our Approach to Flexible Work
 

With Flex Work, we’re combining the best of both worlds: in-person time and remote. Our approach enables our teams to deepen connections, maintain a strong community, and do their best work. We know that flexibility can take shape in many ways, so rather than a number of required days in-office each week, we simply spend at least half (50%) of our time each quarter in the office or in the field with our customers, prospects, and partners (depending on role). This means you'll have the freedom to create a flexible schedule that caters to your business, team, and personal needs, while being intentional to make the most of time spent together. Those in our remote "home office" roles also have the opportunity to come together in our offices for important moments that matter.

At Workday, we are committed to providing an accessible and inclusive hiring experience where all candidates can fully demonstrate their skills. If you require assistance or an accommodation at any point, please email accommodations@workday.com.

Are you being referred to one of our roles? If so, ask your connection at Workday about our Employee Referral process!

At Workday, we value our candidates’ privacy and data security.  Workday will never ask candidates to apply to jobs through websites that are not Workday Careers. 

Please be aware of sites that may ask for you to input your data in connection with a job posting that appears to be from Workday but is not.

In addition, Workday will never ask candidates to pay a recruiting fee, or pay for consulting or coaching services, in order to apply for a job at Workday.
